SPECIES MAP

Herons of the World

Every heron species, mapped to each place it occurs. Each dot is a species in one country, coloured by IUCN Red List status and sized by how many species share that location.

IUCN STATUS KEY

IUCN = International Union for Conservation of Nature; its Red List rates each species' extinction risk. Dot size = number of species sharing that location.

CONSERVATION WATCH



Pick a status to filter the map and this list; click a species to locate it on the map.

Source: IUCN Red List 2025-2 · click a species to locate it on the map

Species Gallery: Final Ardeidae Collection

Explore our curated collection of Ardeidae from around the globe, captured with cinematic precision. Immerse yourself in the spectral beauty of these master hunters.



Portraits are AI-generated and are illustrative only — they may not be accurate and do not reflect a species' real size or scale. For verified photographs and reference imagery, see the Macaulay Library (Cornell Lab of Ornithology).

The Heron Atlas

An interactive documentary experience. © 2026 The Global Heron Atlas.

Citations & Data Sources

Every figure and image in the atlas traces to a cited source below; blanks mean a value is genuinely unrecorded — never invented.



Metadata Density

92.3%

Completeness across 68 species over 8 tracked fields. Blanks are genuine gaps, not to-dos.

Source Mix

Expert authority2
Peer-reviewed dataset2
Citizen-science1
Community3
AI-generated1


AI Imagery & Attribution

Species portraits are generated by Stitch (AI assistant by Google), 2026, for the Heron Atlas project.

While the scientific data — names, status, traits, occurrences — comes from established authorities, the portraits are original AI creations in the "Lumina Atlas" aesthetic, not photographs. Where no portrait exists, the atlas falls back to a real Wikimedia Commons photograph, credited in place.

Derived & Method Notes

  • Residence spots, density, and seasonality are computed from GBIF occurrence records (2006–2025).
  • Migration arcs are a GBIF occurrence approximation — breeding vs non-breeding centroids — not eBird Status & Trends telemetry.
  • Conservation status drives every map dot's colour; blanks are left blank, never inferred.
  • Portraits are AI-generated; where absent, a real Wikimedia Commons photograph is shown and credited in-place.

Data Sources

Tap a card to open the source

9 sources · joined on IOC scientific name


IOC World Bird List v15.2


Expert authority

The taxonomic backbone: supplies every scientific name (the join key across all files), the English common name, and the genus. Nothing links together without it.

68 / 68 speciesFree, w/ attribution


IUCN Red List of Threatened Species


Expert authority

Conservation status (LC→CR) that drives every map dot's colour, plus population trend, population estimate, and the population narrative on each species page.

68 / 68 status · 57 estimatesIUCN Red List terms


GBIF occurrence download


diversity_3Citizen-science

The raw occurrence records behind the whole map: residence spots, the density grid, seasonality, the year×month distribution cube, each species' range centroid, and the GBIF-approximated migration arcs.

10.1M records · 65/68 centroidsMixed (CC0 / BY / BY-NC)


AVONET


Peer-reviewed dataset

Morphological and ecological traits: body mass, habitat and habitat density, dietary/trophic niche, trophic level, primary lifestyle, and migration classification.

65 / 68 speciesCC BY 4.0


EltonTraits 1.0


Peer-reviewed dataset

Foraging ecology: the 5-category diet breakdown, proportional diet composition, and foraging stratum (where in the habitat each species feeds).

63 / 68 speciesFree, w/ citation


Wikidata


Community

Secondary body measurements — length and wingspan in centimetres. (Its mass values were reviewed and rejected as unreliable, so they are not used.)

68 length · 49 wingspanCC0 1.0


Wikimedia Commons


Community

Real species photographs, fetched live via the Wikipedia/Wikimedia REST API for the Gallery cards and as the Species Guide hero when no AI portrait exists. Each photo is credited in place.

Live fetch · variesMixed per-image


OpenStreetMap / CARTO basemap


Community

The basemap tiles beneath the interactive maps — OpenStreetMap map data rendered in CARTO's light 'Voyager' raster style.

Global basemapODbL · © CARTO


The Heron Atlas

Designed for precision, built for discovery. All biological data presented is part of an ongoing open-source initiative to document and preserve the diverse lineage of Ardeidae.
